questions.question: How many stabilizer generators does the Steane [[7,1,3]] code have?
questions.explanation: For an [[n,k,d]] code, there are n-k stabilizer generators. The Steane code has 7-1=6 generators: 3 Z-type (ZIZIZIZ, IZZIIZZ, IIIZZZZ) that detect X errors, and 3 X-type with the same pattern that detect Z errors.

questions.question: What classical code is the Steane code built from?
questions.explanation: The Steane code is built from the Hamming [7,4,3] code using CSS construction with C₁ = C₂ = Hamming. This works because the Hamming code is self-dual-containing (H⊥ ⊆ H).

questions.question: Which transversal gate is NOT directly available on the Steane code?
questions.explanation: The Steane code supports transversal H, CNOT, and all Pauli gates. However, the T gate cannot be implemented transversally and requires magic state injection. This is a general limitation: no code can have transversal gates for a universal set.

questions.question: How many computational basis states appear in |0⟩_L for the Steane code?
questions.explanation: |0⟩_L is a uniform superposition of the 8 even-weight codewords of the Hamming code. |1⟩_L is the superposition of the 8 odd-weight codewords. Together, all 16 Hamming codewords appear.

questions.question: What is the syndrome for an X error on qubit 4 (using the standard Hamming matrix ordering)?
questions.explanation: The syndrome is the binary representation of (qubit+1) in the Hamming convention. Qubit 4 is position 5 in 1-indexed notation, which is 101 in binary, giving syndrome (1,0,1). This is detected by Z-stabilizers g₁ and g₃.

questions.question: Why is the Steane code preferred over the [[5,1,3]] code for fault-tolerant computing?

options.text: It has transversal Hadamard and simpler CSS structure

questions.explanation: The [[5,1,3]] code is more qubit-efficient but the Steane code has practical advantages: transversal Hadamard gate, CSS structure enabling separate X/Z decoding, and transversal CNOT. These make it easier to implement fault-tolerantly.

questions.question: The logical Hadamard gate on the Steane code is implemented by applying physical Hadamard to all 7 qubits.
questions.explanation: True. H_L = H^⊗7 is transversal for the Steane code. This works because the Steane code is self-dual under Hadamard transformation, meaning H^⊗7 maps codewords to codewords while implementing the logical Hadamard.
